description_long: This object is a medium-sized airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a smooth, light-colored exterior. The wings are attached at a mid-level position on the fuselage and appear to be straight with a slight dihedral angle, lacking visible winglets. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned at the forward end of the fuselage and is enclosed, with windows visible in the front view. The surface of the airplane is predominantly white with some gray and subtle markings, and the overall structure suggests a typical passenger or small commercial aircraft. The landing gear is not clearly visible, but the general form and proportions are consistent with a standard urban-compatible airplane.
description: A streamlined, light-colored airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and a forward enclosed cockpit. The surface is mostly white with some gray and subtle markings.
description_view_0: The left side of the airplane is visible, showing the mid-mounted wings and part of the cockpit area, with the tail section partially obscured.
description_view_1: The rear-left angle highlights the tail section and the dihedral angle of the wings, with the fuselage tapering toward the back.
description_view_2: The front view displays the nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical placement of the wings, with the tail visible in the background.
description_view_3: The rear-right angle emphasizes the tail assembly and the right wing, with the fuselage narrowing toward the tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and a slight dihedral angle, no visible winglets or variable geometry.
attribute_engine_type: Engines not clearly visible in the images.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not clearly visible in the images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with visible windows.
